title: Podman is not working in rootless k8s container when privilege flag set to false
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
I am trying to run rootless podman in kubernetes container with setting privilege set to true.

-->

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Podman is not running in k8s container with root user as well non root user if privilege is not set true. Even basic pod man command like **podman info** is failing. This issue is blocking us to move podman into production 
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create kubernetes pod with podman image when privilege is not set true

2. Run Podman CLI commnads like podman info


**Describe the results you received:**

/app # podman info
ERRO[0006] 'overlay' is not supported over extfs at "/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay"
Error: kernel does not support overlay fs: 'overlay' is not supported over extfs at "/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay": backing file system is unsupported for this graph driver

**Describe the results you expected:**
Pod man should worked in k8s container with privilege set to false

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
   podman version 3.0.1
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**
Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
AKS machine with alpine base image

comments.body: @rhatdan Any update on this, this security concern is blocking us to use podman for our production use case
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@SahilChaudhary25 It seems like your setup is using native overlay on `privileged: false`. However podman within unprivileged scenario relies on `fuse-overlayfs` instead of `native overlay`.

You need to configure device plugin `/dev/fuse` and use that within your pod spec https://github.com/kuberenetes-learning-group/fuse-device-plugin/blob/main/fuse-device-plugin-k8s-1.16.yml.

Read more about using running k8s pods with `fuse-overlayfs` setup here : https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-inside-kubernetes

Closing this issue since `privileged` and `native overlay` together is not supported. cc @giuseppe Could you please confirm if we support `native overlay` under unprivileged scenarios since i am not 100% sure .
